The following properties are made available for use in the Humanoid Luau VM:
  - WalkSpeed

Merry christmas eve, RbxCli will likely decrease updates until 2026 (Or until I decide to work again).

- Extended `memory` Luau VM library with new reading and writing extensions instead of plain buffer objects.
- Added: `memory.readu8`, `memory.readi8`, `memory.readbool`, `memory.readu16`, `memory.readi16`, `memory.readu32`, `memory.readi32`, `memory.readf32`, `memory.readf64`, `memory.read_pointer` (This function may fail if the read value is irrepresentable within the limits of a double precision IEEE-754 float, which isn't normally the case, but it may happen, so note it), `memory.writeu8`, `memory.writei8`, `memory.writebool`, `memory.writeu16`, `memory.writei16`, `memory.writeu32`, `memory.writei32`, `memory.writef32`, `memory.writef64`.
- Added `physics` Luau VM library containing physics-related functions, such as `raycast`. These functions are yet to be documented.
- Fix crash that happened within the NPC system due to type confusion.
- Visibility checking now optionally checks transparency, this setting can be modified in the Settings.
(This is due to some games utilizing custom materials which conflict with the standard transparency, causing false transparent parts, which lead to in some cases entire maps being discarded (Defuse Division, Reminisence Zombies, particularly on the Backrooms map).
- Mitigate bounding box errors by only taking into account parts considered 'In Movement'.

- Fixed saving Hitbox Expander Settings.
- Fixed saving 'Draw Aimbot Field of View Circle'.
- Added back load failures for Kernel Mode Driver loading, including erroring states.
- Added label to Flight Speed dragger, previously missing.
- Improved auth performance.
- Reduced cracking false positives.
- Admin is now **required** to boot RbxCli.
- Fixed heap corruption product of multithreaded ImGui usage.
- Optimized access to some Workspace properties.
- Slightly improved memory reading performance, code size may have been increased. (Very slight, product of branch re-ordering)

This is a hotfix release.
- Changed mesh normalization to be per-axis instead of general. This should completely solve malformed meshes, product of scaling based on all axis instead of per-axis. (Assuming Uniform meshes instead of Non-Uniform ones, which isn't the case for most meshes)
- (Implicitly) Fixed mesh-based chameleon rendering (Which sometimes resulted in broken parts or malformed parts).
- (Implicitly) Fixed mesh visibility checking being imprecise on some meshes.
- (Implicitly) Fixed wireframe view for meshes inside of the Physics World Debug View.

To obtain the benefits of this update you MUST purge all files inside your `RbxCli/Meshes` folder before using RbxCli again, this clears the mesh cache and allows RbxCli to download those meshes again.

- Added `UDim2` library: `UDim2.new`, `UDim2.fromScale`, `UDim2.fromOffset`, fully Roblox API compliant.
- Added `UDim` library: `UDim.new`, fully Roblox API compliant.
- Added `UDim` object, fully Roblox API compliant.
- Added `UDim2` object, fully Roblox API compliant.
- Added `GuiObject` to Luau VM, `Size` and `Position` properties are supported.
- Added `TextLabel` to Luau VM, `Text` property is supported.
- Fixed infinitely growing list set, which slowed down the Luau VM over time due to bad list management.
- Added `NetworkOwnerV3` to `BasePart` objects to obtain the Owner's Network ID, you can use our documented Network IDs on the Instance Explorer to know what the value represents (Formal documentation coming soon)
  - -1 => Not Simulated
  - 1 => Server/Automatic Ownership
  - 2 => Welded/Anchored
  - 3 => Remotely Simulated (Player/Server)
  - 4+ => Locally Simulated (This value is your Network ID)
- Fixed deadlock present on RbxCli's exit after using the Luau VM.
- Fixed mis-documented Network ID on explorer.

Formal documentation to new APIs will be releasing soon, it is just a lot to write and honestly, I'm not willing to sit down and write the 20+ new functions and design the ones that I'm missing, such as Drawing.

- Expanded `physics` library to encompass more of the internal native `RbxCli::PhysicsWorld` strucutre it exposes to the Luau VM, currently it is as follows:
  - `physics.get_part_pool(part: Instance<BasePart>): number`: Retrieves the 'pool' the part is located in inside of RbxCli. This changes the update rate on the overlay's visuals and affects how 'up to date' data is when using other physics functions.
  - `physics.is_part_tracked(part: Instance<BasePart>): boolean`: Retrieves whether the part is actually being updated inside of RbxCli, parts inside of Characters are normally dismissed and may return `false` for this method, it may also return false if the part is very new, ~20 seconds time new.
  - `physics.get_tracked_size(part: Instance<BasePart>): Vector3`: Retrieves the read 'Size' of the given part from the last Update RbxCli performed on it. This does not perform a read from Roblox memory, thus it WILL be quicker, but may be out of date depending on the part's pool.
  - `physics.get_tracked_cframe(part: Instance<BasePart>): CFrame`: Retrieves the read 'CFrame' of the given part from the last Update RbxCli performed on it. This does not perform a read from Roblox memory, thus it WILL be quicker, but may be out of date depending on the part's pool.
  - `physics.can_part_be_tracked(part: Instance<BasePart>): boolean`: Retrieves whether the given part was actually disabled from being Updated, explicitly set by RbxCli's PhysicsWorld.

Fission has been improved significantly this update.
- Added `require` Path-based renaming
- Added property-based renaming
  - i.e. say you access `v0.LocalPlayer`, the variable that it is stored into will be named `localPlayer`—just good old realistic name propagation.
- Added `GetXXXXXXX` based renaming, where 'xXXXXXX' will become the name of the variable should it be available 
- Improved reliability when lifting empty loop bodies, which lead to the CFA to confuse itself and begin breaking
- Improved lifting on table expressions.
- Improved AST generation times.
- Rewrote part of the renaming pipeline.
- Improved reliability of renaming pipeline—if a name collides with an existing global (say, `table`, `game`, ...) then the rewrite will be **rejected** and the variable will remain on it's normal, `vX` normal name.
- Implemented property assignment back-propagation.
  - i.e. say you write to `vN.propertyName` with a variable, fission will now automatically promote the variable from `vN` to `propertyName`.
- Improved data flow analysis for loops and branching.
